svg.text: 01 - Journey Buddi Master Map (Read First)
Purpose: full-system orientation. Read this first, then continue in numeric order.
Mission + Value Proposition
Journey Buddi is an AI-powered adaptive travel companion for extended nature/adventure trips.
Core value: AI-guided planning before travel + real-time condition-aware adaptation during travel.
Pilot destination: New Zealand (high weather volatility, diverse activities, ideal stress test for adaptive logic).
Product Experience Model
Phase A - Planning (conversation-driven):
1) profile/preferences -> 2) destination/dates -> 3) must-dos
4) interest deep-dive -> 5) attraction curation -> 6) route build
7) transport -> 8) pace/duration -> 9) itinerary dashboard -> 10) refine
Phase B - Live Companion:
Evening preview, morning briefing, live updates, day wrap.
Condition scoring: EXCELLENT / GOOD / FAIR / POOR / UNSAFE.
Swap suggestions only when improvement is meaningful and feasible.
Cross-cutting: chat, map, notifications, hidden gems, stories, account persistence.
Design principles: conversation-first, calm design, glanceable-then-deep.
Primary user context: mobile during trip; desktop/tablet during planning.
Deep dive files: 02, 03, 10
AI + Intelligence Model
Agents: Planning Agent, Live Companion Agent, General Chat Agent.
Primary model: Gemini; fallback/comparison: GPT/Claude for resiliency.
Tool-calling accesses weather, tides, solar, maps, itinerary, profile, RAG knowledge.
Decision domains:
- attraction selection (interest+fitness+route+budget+uniqueness)
- location/provider selection, route sequencing, timing, packing
- swap evaluation (improvement vs disruption)
RAG architecture: destination knowledge chunks -> embeddings -> vector search -> prompt grounding.
Guardrails: schema validation, confidence levels, source-grounded safety data.
Decision logging supports explainability, debugging, and iterative improvement.
Deep dive file: 03
Engineering Architecture Model
Frontend: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind + React Query + Zustand + Mapbox.
Backend: FastAPI async modular monolith (decomposable later).
Data layer: PostgreSQL/PostGIS + Redis + Chroma (MVP) -> Pinecone (scale).
Background + real-time: Celery beat/workers, Redis broker, WebSocket chat streaming.
Auth: magic link + JWT (passwordless).
APIs: REST /api/v1 + WS /api/v1/ws/chat/:conversationId.
External integrations normalized to canonical schemas with fallback chains.
Deployment MVP: Railway (app + postgres + redis + celery worker).
Key objective: ship quickly while preserving clean service boundaries.
Deep dive files: 04, 05, 06, 07
System-Wide Data and Control Loop (End-to-End)
1) User interacts with Buddi in planning or companion mode -> request hits FastAPI API layer.
2) Service layer orchestrates AI, itinerary logic, condition evaluation, and retrieval from structured DB + vector KB.
3) External Data Service fetches weather/tides/solar/maps from providers with per-provider rate limits and circuit breakers.
4) Adapter layer normalizes heterogeneous payloads into canonical models (consistent units, UTC timestamps, schema-safe fields).
5) Multi-tier cache (memory/Redis/Postgres) serves hot reads fast; stale policy controls degraded scenarios.
6) Condition scoring compares forecast vectors against activity condition profiles; confidence scales by forecast horizon.
7) If major mismatch is detected, swap engine computes candidate moves and disruption impacts; Buddi proposes only high-value changes.
8) Celery tasks prefetch and refresh conditions, generate briefings on schedule, and trigger push notifications by user timezone.
9) User accepts/declines changes; itinerary versions, decisions, and outcomes are persisted for transparency and continuous tuning.
10) UX layer keeps all this complex logic calm and actionable (quick-glance summaries + deep expandable context).
This loop is the product moat: static trip planners cannot perform this continuous adaptation cycle.
Recommended Reading Order (by SVG File)
01 - Master map (this file): full orientation and architecture of the architecture.
02 - Product planning + live companion flows: user value and operational behavior.
03 - AI agent + decision engine: how Buddi reasons, retrieves knowledge, and chooses actions.
04 - System architecture runtime: services, communication patterns, deployment shape.
05 - API + WebSocket + events: interface contracts and real-time interaction protocol.
06 - Data model + geospatial schema: persistence foundations and core entities.
07 - External integrations + caching + fallback logic: operational resilience and costs.
08 - NZ pilot intelligence: domain rules Buddi needs to behave like a real local expert.
09 - Roadmap + implementation sequence: what to build first and what "done" means per phase.
10 - UX information architecture: interaction patterns, hierarchy, and emotional quality bar.
Implementation note: files 03-07 are the highest-priority technical set for coding.
Domain note: file 08 is critical for correct NZ decision quality (especially weather/tide windows).
Delivery note: this pack synthesizes the docs corpus into actionable system views, not just summaries.
